steps.text: Establish the base rate: the tree grows 51 cm every $m$ months, so the rate is $51/m$ cm per month.
Convert $k$ years to months: $k$ years $\times 12$ months/year $= 12k$ months.
Multiply rate by time: growth in $k$ years $= \dfrac{51}{m} \times 12k = \dfrac{612k}{m}$ centimeters.

• Choice A — $\frac{17m}{4k}$: has $m$ in the numerator and $k$ in the denominator, inverted from what we need, and the constant is wrong. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — $\frac{17k}{4m}$: simplifies to $612k/(48m)$, not $612k/m$. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — $\frac{612m}{k}$: $m$ and $k$ are swapped relative to our result. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — $\frac{612k}{m}$: matches our derived expression exactly. That confirms D is correct.
